notes: iter21 (added project_field(name) event + short reasoning_encrypted_content='x' placeholder, hypothesis: FE checks presence not content of reasoning_encrypted_content) — IDENTICAL failure mode to iter19/20: after submit, FE first shows the refresh banner ('변경 사항을 적용하려면 새로고침이 필요합니다.' / '새로고침' button) and ~15s later 'Failed to fetch' plain-text appears below the user bubble. Hypothesis DISPROVEN: presence-only of reasoning_encrypted_content (value='x') is insufficient — either the FE validates the contents (must be valid encrypted payload / base64 / expected length), OR project_field(name) shape is wrong, OR another axis is still missing. Notable detail vs iter19/20: this run the refresh banner appeared FIRST and 'Failed to fetch' surfaced later (~18s after submit) instead of simultaneously — possibly because the new project_field(name) event made the FE start consuming the stream further before throwing. Engine accepted payload (no upstream reject). Warning text '차단' never rendered. Recommend next axis: (a) inspect HAR for actual reasoning_encrypted_content payload shape/encoding from a real upstream genspark response and mimic byte-for-byte (raise budget), (b) verify project_field event shape (key names, ordering vs message_result), (c) consider parser may require a specific terminator other than what we inject. Workflow note: had to minimize concurrent Chrome windows ('Result of one plus one - Chrome' with DevTools, '새 탭', 'Claude') because they kept stealing focus and OS-level keystroke injection failed silently; final approach used windows-mcp Snapshot DOM detection (input id=0) + Type tool which works reliably once target window is the sole maximized Chrome window.
